title: taraxacum pollichii
intro: Taraxacum pollichii, also known as Pollich's dandelion, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, closely related to the common dandelion (Taraxacum officinale) but with distinct differences in its leaves and flowers. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum pollichii lie in its roots and leaves, which are rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like potassium and iron. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and liver and kidney disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Taraxacum pollichii can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
